title: Fix resuming after `ds.set_epoch(new_epoch)`
body:
close https://github.com/huggingface/datasets/issues/7447

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Fix `IterableDataset` checkpoint/resume semantics when `set_epoch(new_epoch)` is used by making epoch changes propagate correctly and by making iterable checkpoint state epoch-aware (or invalidated) so resuming cannot silently skip/duplicate examples across epoch boundaries.

steps.operation: Update epoch propagation and invalidate/realign resume state when epoch changes
steps.reason: `set_epoch(new_epoch)` changes the deterministic ordering/seed used by shuffling. If an iterator has already progressed (or a state_dict was produced) under a previous epoch, resuming with a different epoch can produce skips/duplicates unless the epoch change is explicitly propagated and any existing checkpoint state is either made epoch-aware or invalidated/reset.
steps.side_effects: May change user-visible semantics: calling `set_epoch` after partial iteration may reset progress (or require re-loading state for the same epoch). Could impact any wrappers that rely on mutating epoch without restarting iteration; requires updating/adding tests to codify the contract.

steps.operation: Persist epoch identity (or derived per-epoch RNG identity) in the iterable checkpoint state
steps.reason: To make resume deterministic, the checkpoint must capture enough information to guarantee the same sample order on restore. If epoch affects shuffling seed/order, the saved state must include the epoch (or a per-epoch seed/ordering identifier) so `load_state_dict` can detect mismatches and behave predictably.
steps.side_effects: Changes the serialized shape of iterable state dicts; older checkpoints may still load if handled compatibly, but downstream code/tests that assert exact keys may need updates.

steps.operation: Initialize epoch-aware state fields when creating a fresh state dict
steps.reason: When starting iteration (or when first constructing checkpoint state), initialize epoch-related metadata in the state structure so subsequent `state_dict()` calls and nested iterables have a consistent place to store epoch identity and any per-epoch RNG bookkeeping.
steps.side_effects: If nested iterables override/extend state initialization, they may need to be compatible with the new field(s). Might require adjusting assumptions in subclasses about the minimal state schema.

steps.operation: Validate epoch compatibility on restore and define behavior for mismatched epoch (reset/reseed/recompute)
steps.reason: If a state dict saved under epoch E is loaded while the dataset/iterable is configured for epoch E', restoring raw counters/buffer state can be invalid. `load_state_dict` should detect the mismatch (via the epoch field added to the state) and either (a) refuse with a clear error, or (b) reset/realign state in a documented way (commonly: reset progress for the new epoch, or require `set_epoch(E)` before loading).
steps.side_effects: Could raise new exceptions in previously silent misuses; may force callers to adjust ordering of calls (e.g., call `set_epoch` before `load_state_dict`). Tests must cover both matching-epoch resume and mismatched-epoch behavior.
